title: All Souls' Solemn Requiem
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver
## November 2, 2024 | "All Souls' Solemn Requiem"

LITURGICAL CONTEXT
November 2 is All Souls' Day in the Christian calendar, observed one day after All Saints' Day (November 1). In the Episcopal Church using the Revised Common Lectionary, this date typically features readings focused on resurrection and the communion of saints, often including passages from Wisdom 3:1-9 and John 6:37-40. The sermon falls in Ordinary Time, the longest liturgical season, between Pentecost and Advent, creating a reflective moment before the approach of the Christmas/Advent season beginning December 1.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS
The Episcopal Church was navigating post-General Convention adjustments following the 77th General Convention in Louisville, Kentucky (June 2024), which had addressed several contentious issues including sexuality and identity resolutions. The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Glasspool's leadership, was engaged in ongoing conversations about inclusion and pastoral care. No major diocesan elections or crises were active in early November 2024, though the national church continued processing the ramifications of the summer's legislative decisions.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS
Denver experienced typical early November weather with temperatures in the 40s-50s Fahrenheit and possible light precipitation, consistent with Colorado's transitional autumn period. No major municipal crises dominated local headlines in the final week of October 2024. The Capitol Hill neighborhood, where Saint John's Cathedral is located, was preparing for the holiday season with the city's typical downtown lighting preparations underway.

NATIONAL NEWS
The 2024 U.S. presidential election was held on November 5, 2024—just three days after this sermon—creating a heightened political atmosphere across the nation. Donald Trump and Kamala Harris were in their final campaign days with intensive get-out-the-vote efforts. The election dominated American news cycles, creating underlying social tension and deep concern in many congregations about national divisions and future direction.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS
The Israel-Gaza conflict remained active and devastating, now in its second year following October 7, 2023, with significant humanitarian crises and international diplomatic efforts ongoing. The Ukraine-Russia war continued with winter approaching, affecting energy crises across Europe. Both conflicts were sources of serious moral and pastoral concern for many mainline Protestant congregations.

CULTURAL MOMENT
The nation existed in a state of pre-election anxiety and division, with many Americans experiencing genuine uncertainty and concern about the immediate future. All Souls' Day's traditional themes of remembrance and mortality held particular resonance in a moment when many people felt fragile about the state of the nation and world. The reflective, memorial nature of November 2 created a countercultural moment of stillness before electoral intensity.
